I will briefly update you on the progress work Safe has made in delivering the government's health and safety reform priorities during 20 24, 25. And then take your questions As part of a broader suite of health and safety reforms, the government is refocusing work safe from an enforcement agency to one that engages early to support businesses and individuals to manage their critical risks.

These reforms are essential to creating safer workplaces and ensuring regulation is proportionate, practical, and consistent. WorkSafe has undertaken a significant strategic reset. The organization is embedding a risk-based enforcement model, refreshing sector specific guidance, and improving permitting.

Systems Enforcement is shifting from reactive compliance to proportionate educated engagement. Guidance for high harm industries has been refreshed, outdated, materials removed, and new tools piloted to improve accessibility. These give businesses greater certainty about what is reasonably practicable and supports the shift to a more proportionate educative regulator.

WorkSafe trajectory is positive. Operationally, backlogs and permitting applications have been significantly reduced. And Inspectorate capability uplift is underway through new training and performance frameworks. In the past year, WorkSafe completed over 13,000 assessments, a 8.3% increase on the previous year, reflecting its intent to be more visible and proactive, particularly in high harm sectors and financially.

WorkSafe has built strong reserves that can be used to support a multi-year strategic work program, ensuring reform delivery without creating ongoing baseline pressure. I have recently strengthened the governance of the organization by appointing a new chair and two new board members. These appointments follow an earlier refresh of board skills in 2024.

The current board understands what is required to support the government's reforms and strengthen work safes role as a proportionate and practical regulator. In summary, WorkSafe is making tangible progress with strong governance, clear strategy, and a commitment to proportionate and practical regulation.

I'm confident WorkSafe is well positioned to deliver the outcomes we expect and support safer workplaces across New Zealand in the coming years. And I'm very happy to take your questions.
